title: Jewish Studio Project
description: Jewish Studio Project at Coastal Roots Farm Join us for a guided experience with the Jewish Studio Process—an approach that combines Jewish learning, creative exploration, and personal reflection. Through text, conversation, and hands-on materials, we’ll open to a deeper encounter with beauty: not just how things appear, but how they move us, stir us, call us closer to what matters. No artistic experience is needed—just a spirit of curiosity and a willingness to play.

description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity.

title: Harvest to Table Culinary Experience
description: Join us for an morning of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. No prior cooking experience is needed—just a love for good food and great company.

Jewish tradition teaches that everything exists in relationship: we don’t exist in isolation but rather as part of a larger, interconnected whole. This year’s theme “Together we Grow” reminds us of the profound power of this connection.

Class will be framed by Jewish wisdom. with time to share and ask questions. People of any or no faith background are always welcome.

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms
description: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms Following the success of a fall class, guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman returns to the Farm for a joyful, cross-cultural exploration of Qigong & the Poetry of Tehillim— Psalms in Hebrew. In this six-part series, we will bring together two rich traditions in order to enhance physical and spiritual health. With its gentle movements and guided breathing, Qigong will help us to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies.

title: Havdalah on the Farm
description: Havdalah on the Farm Havdalah, literally meaning separation, is a ritual held at the end of Shabbat, after the sun has set and when three stars appear. There are blessings over wine, a candle, and spices. It is a ritual that allows us to hold onto the sweet moments of Shabbat for just a few more minutes before going into our busy week. Join Coastal Roots Farm and The Hive on Saturday, February 19th from 7 - 8:30 PM on the Farm for a special Havdalah service led by Sharone Oren and Ashly Tamayo.

title: Winter Gardening Workshop
description: Join us for a special Winter Gardening Workshop: Tree Pruning. Learn with Christina Cranwell, our experienced Outdoor Educator who has been farming 5+ years across multiple states. There is no experience necessary to join, gloves and materials provided. Cover all the basics of tree maintenance to promote healthy tree form, fruiting, and learn how to choose the right pruning tool to protect the longevity of your fruit tree.

title: Bio Blitz on the Farm
description: Join the search for hidden species on the Farm! A bio blitz is an event where people gather to identify and record as many species of plants and animals as possible within an area. This citizen-science initiative will help the farm team better understand, protect, and promote ecological diversity on the Farm. Join us on April 18th from 2:00 pm to 4:00 pm, for our first Bio Blitz on the farm. No experience is necessary to participate, children and families are welcome.

title: Plant. Prepare. Pray: Shavuot Learning and Celebration
description: Shavuot, the Jewish holiday that commemorates the giving of the Torah on Mount Sinai, is also a deeply agricultural celebration. In ancient Israel, it marked the time when farmers brought bikkurim—the first fruits of their harvest—to Jerusalem as a gesture of gratitude and connection. After offering their choicest fruits and engaging in sacred reconnection, they returned home to enjoy the bounty of their labor.

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

title: From Harvest to Table, A Cooking Class on the Farm

Earth-based Jewish wisdom will ground the event and serve as inspiration and jumping-off point for the artists. As we all navigate the darkness of the climate crisis and the environmental injustices of today, art can light our way and help us see, feel, and act with renewed joy and determination. That’s our hope for this Fest.

title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 7/25 at Sun Runner Lot
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
location: Sun Runner Lot, San Diego, CA
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA
description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! title: Earth Based Judaism: Talmud, Land, and Belonging (3-Part Series)
description: In this three-session cohort , we will gather at the Farm to explore Earth-Based Judaism through Talmudic stories about creativity, home, drought, rain, and the relationship between human beings and the natural world. Together, we will read ancient texts through literary, cultural, and contemporary lenses, paying attention to the landscapes that shape them: caves, streams, trees, homes, dry earth, and falling rain.

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
description: Savor the Harvest at the August 9 Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Urban Kitchen Group . Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful pairings of wine and beer.

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity.

title: Outdoor Mandala Making Workshop
description: Mandala is Sanskrit for “circle” and is most commonly associated with Hindu and Buddhist cultures. Join the Farm’s Director of Jewish Life, Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a hands-on, Jewish take on outdoor mandala-making, in commemoration of the upcoming Jewish fast day of Tisha B'Av. Make your own unique mandala using organic materials from the Farm’s Food Forest and engage in a conversation about the many ways we can love -- freely and abundantly.

title: Birding & Brews
location: San Diego Bay Refuge, Imperial Beach, CA
description: Be an Ocean Connector and start your day with birding and brews! Sip on smooth, nitro cold brew from Trident Coffee as you learn how their values of connection, community, and character align with our conservation mission. Then, head out on a guided walking tour along the Bayshore Bikeway at South San Diego Bay, equipped with binoculars and field guides. What to Expect: Coffee Tasting: Kick off the tour with a unique nitro cold brew tasting, crafted locally by Trident Coffee.

title: Eagle Presentation
location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA
description: Join us at Eagle Mesa for a presentation about our bald & golden eagles! Learn about these two non-releasable raptors, like what they eat, why they reside at the Living Coast, fun facts, and more! *All events subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA
description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike.

title: Shark & Ray Presentation
location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA
description: Visit the Shark & Ray Experience to learn about the diverse range of species that inhabit it, including leopard sharks, bat rays, diamond rays, shovelnose guitarfish, horn sharks, and more! This exhibit features a touch tank where you can interact with some of these residents while learning about them. *All presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of the staff.

title: Sea Turtle Presentation
location: Living Coast Discovery Center, Chula Vista, CA
description: Join us at Turtle Lagoon to learn about green sea turtles, even the ones you can find right here in San Diego Bay! A Living Coast educator will tell you all about sea turtles, like their diet, shell, fun facts, and more! *All animal presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

title: Sensory Friendly Mornings
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Join us every Sunday from 9:00 a.m. - 10:30 a.m. for early mornings geared for families with children on the spectrum or with sensory sensitivities: Join a welcoming, comfortable environment Enjoy the Museum in a quiet and accepting setting Make connections and share resources with other families Free with Museum admission.

title: Tishrei Ag Fest
description: The cycle of agriculture requires that we continually regenerate, repair, and renew in order to grow. Likewise, the Hebrew month of Tishrei, often referred to as the “month of beginning,” is the first month of the Jewish year and reminds us that such renewal is possible at the human level too: with each new year, we reflect where we have been and where we’re going so that we may begin again with intention.

title: Watershed Warriors: Ramona
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the County of San Diego for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. Our staff will have check-in ready for you on-site. Be sure to wear comfortable clothes, closed-toed shoes, some form of sun protection (sunscreen or a hat), and bring a water bottle or two! Staff will be providing everything else you will need! Click the register below for more information and to get signed up!

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
location: Bayside Park, Chula Vista
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

title: SOLD OUT: Farm-to-Table Dinner Featuring Mark Enciso
description: Savor the Harvest at the September Farm-to-Table Dinner featuring Mark Enciso from Order Fire Barbecue Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by featured Chef Mark Enciso from Order Fire Barbecue. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

title: Cultivating Impact Mixer
description: We invite you to join us for refreshments and music while you connect with community members committed to caring for people and planet. Cultivating Impact Mixer September 15th, 4:30-7:30pm Coastal Roots Farm 441 Saxony Rd Encinitas CA, 92007 The event will take place in the Farm's Field 11 event space. Food and refreshments will be provided. Register Here Thank you to our Series Presenting Sponsor Thank you to our In-Kind Sponsors

title: Farm Film and Music Series
description: Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet. We Can Get There From Here: After years studying marine microplastics around the globe, Abby Barrows was ready to come home. In 2015, looking for a side-project that would keep her on the water, she bought the lease for an oyster farm in Deer Isle, Maine.

title: 42nd Annual Coastal Cleanup Day
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: The Ocean Conservancy and the California Coastal Commission partner with volunteer organizations, such as I Love A Clean San Diego to create the largest, international cleanup day. I Love A Clean San Diego leads the efforts for this day in San Diego County for the 42nd year in a row. Data collected from this event feeds into the world’s largest database on marine debris.

description: A New Year for Action! This first gathering coincides with Sun-Day, a national day of action for clean energy.

description: Experience Film, Music, and Meaningful Conversation on the Farm Join us on the Farm for an evening of community, live music, and transformative storytelling. location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Discover new, furry friends with different animal partners every month! Pre-registration is not required. Free with Museum admission. We recommend arriving at the Museum early to maximize your chances of meeting the animals. A Hold Harmless form is required to be filled out for all guests who want to pet the animals.

description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! title: Farm Film & Music Series: Tribal Waters
description: Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet. Thursday, September 28th - Tribal Waters directed by Jon Klaczkiewicz, a film by Teton Gravity Research presented by Patagonia, documents the course and history of the magnificent Wind River, now the home waters of the Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho Tribes.

title: Harvest to Table Cooking Class
description: Join us for an evening of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. Through this hands-on experience, you’ll have the chance to harvest fresh produce, learn new culinary skills, and share a nourishing meal with fellow participants.

title: Nature Play Rosh Hashanah
description: Come celebrate a sweet Jewish New Year with us at Nature Play! Join us for a fun-filled morning on the Farm, complete with Rosh Hashanah-themed arts and crafts, story time, songs, and more delightful surprises! $8 per child and $8 for the first adult. Click to learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 10/3 at Sun Runner Lot
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
location: Sun Runner Lot, San Diego, CA
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

title: Queer in the Sukkah
description: Thursday, October 5 @ 6:00 pm to 8:00 pm - Preregistration is required. No walk-ups will be allowed. You are invited to Coastal Roots Farm’s first-ever queer gathering and party in celebration of Sukkot, the Jewish Harvest Festival sometimes referred to as "the time of our joy." Soak in the magic of our farm; experience joyful Sukkot traditions; get inspired by Hanz Enyeart, Queer Jewish scholar and writer (check out his column in The Rage magazine!) who will invite in the stories of...

We're screening the premiere of The Threads Between Us.

title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2022
description: Join us for the 10th Annual Sukkot Harvest Festival at Coastal Roots Farm! The Sukkot Harvest Festival is the Farm's biggest event of the year, inspired by the Jewish tradition of gathering community outdoors to celebrate the end of an abundant summer harvest and welcome in the fall season and all it brings. Meet us under the sukkah (temporary shelter) for a day filled with live music, food, and activities for all ages.

description: Sukkot Shabbat Dinner Join Director of Jewish Life Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a celebration of community, soil, and soul in this Shabbat and Sukkot dinner celebration inside the Farm sukkah! Connect with friends new and old, share stories of abundance and harvest, and engage in Sukkot learning and ritual which bind us more closely to love of the earth and its bounty. There will be opportunity to meet, laugh, and sing! No need to identify as Jewish to thoroughly enjoy.

title: Litter Free La Mesa: La Mesa
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of La Mesa for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. All supplies are provided. Click the registration button below for more information and to get signed up!

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
location: Bayside Park, Chula Vista
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2025
description: Festival Activities Sponsors Tickets When it comes to celebrating the earth, ancient Jewish tradition recommends we do it together. Coastal Roots Farm invites you to celebrate with us at our 13th annual Sukkot Harvest Festival, our biggest event of the year! This intergenerational event features live music, local food, hands-on workshops, and engaging activities for all ages as we celebrate the bounty of our Farm and the spirit of our diverse community.

title: Pride in the Sukkah
description: LGBTQ+ Sukkot Celebration at Coastal Roots Farm Come celebrate the beauty of LGBTQ+ community and the harvest holiday of Sukkot under Coastal Roots Farm’s big sukkah! Together we’ll gather with friends old and new, share festive food and drink, and explore the meaning of Sukkot through Earth Based Judaism learning. Sukkot is one of the most communal Jewish holidays. In the sukkah, a traditional temporary outdoor structure, we leave the walls open so that everyone feels welcome.

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & Psalms
description: Qigong & Psalms Join Coastal Roots Farm and guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman, Director of Spiritual Life at Jewish Family Service, for an embodied exploration of Qigong & Psalms. In this four-part, interactive series, we will draw on qigong to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies while connecting more deeply to the imagery and inspiration found in the ancient Book of Psalms.

title: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle
description: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. What is Dayenu?Dayenu is a national Jewish movement responding to the climate crisis with hope, action, and community. Local Dayenu Circles bring people together to learn, connect, and take meaningful steps toward a more just and sustainable future. You don’t need to identify as Jewish to participate—everyone is welcome.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
description: Savor the Harvest at the October Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Chef Alex Velasco. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings. With a 60-guest maximum, this experience ensures a cozy and communal atmosphere.

title: The Big Birthday Block Party
description: Enjoy free admission to the Museum, explore all exhibits, a 21+ rooftop bar, enjoy food, drink, music, deals, and more.

title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2024
description: When it comes to celebrating the earth, ancient Jewish tradition recommends we do it together. Coastal Roots Farm invites you to celebrate with us at our 12th annual Sukkot Harvest Festival, our biggest event of the year! This intergenerational event features live music, local food, hands-on workshops, and engaging activities for all ages as we celebrate the bounty of our Farm and the spirit of our diverse community.

title: SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering
description: The SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering at Coastal Roots Farm is an opportunity for Tribal members in Southern California that are actively cultivating or interested in joining the food sovereignty movement among Indigenous Nations. Examples of people active in food sovereignty work include Tribal farmers, seed keepers, land stewards, hunters, livestock owners, foragers, botanists, fishers, medicine makers, and cooks.

description: Dine with us during an intimate Farm-to-Table Dinner at Coastal Roots Farm! Featuring Chef Francesco Bonsi Sicilian-born, locally-based personal chef and food educator rooted in supporting local farms and giving guests a true taste of Italy in each meal that he creates. Including seasonal, organic farm-fresh produce. Supporting the Farm's impact in the community.

title: Intro to Earth-Based Judaism Series (4 Classes)
description: In this four-part pilot class, we will gather in lovely locations across the Farm to explore 4 basic pathways into the spiritual revolution called Earth-Based Judaism -- and how to bring those pathways back into your life. The goal is more resilience, earth-love, responsibility, and joy. Our learning will include sacred Jewish texts and wisdom, hearty conversation, on-our-feet exploration and play, and moments of quiet reflection. We'll also provide coffee, pastries, and time to schmooze.

title: Farm Film and Music Series
description: Experience Film, Music, and Meaningful Conversation on the Farm Join us on the Farm for an evening of community, live music, and transformative storytelling. Part of our 5-part Farm Film & Music Series, this event invites guests to gather under the stars and engage in inspiring dialogue about regenerative agriculture, environmental justice, and the future of our food systems. We're screening two documentaries: Faka'apa'apa and an early draft release of Out of Mined (coming March 2026).

title: KPBS Kids Event: Elinor Wonders Why
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Follow your curiosity and ask questions with Elinor from Elinor Wonders Why! title: Compost and Mulch Giveaway – 10/31 at Los Penasquitos Ranch House
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
location: Los Penasquitos Ranch House, San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

title: Intro to Earth-Based Judaism Series (4 classes)
description: In this four-part pilot class, we will gather in lovely locations across the Farm to explore 4 basic pathways into the spiritual revolution called Earth-Based Judaism -- and how to bring those pathways back into your life. The goal is more resilience, earth-love, responsibility, and joy. Our learning will include sacred Jewish texts and wisdom, hearty conversation, on-our-feet exploration and play, and moments of quiet reflection. We'll also provide coffee, pastries, and time to schmooze.

title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 11/14 at Sun Runner Lot
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
location: Sun Runner Lot, San Diego, CA
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

title: Havdalah on the Farm
description: Join us in celebrating Havdalah on the Farm and mark the end of Shabbat! Spend time in the great outdoors and gather in community to usher in the new week. Activities include: making a farm-fresh besamim (spice bag) and tasting local wines. Plus! A chance to make your own locally-sourced beeswax candle. Havdalah ceremony will be led by Camp Mountain Chai's musical specialist, Marshall Voit followed by dancing and music. Family friendly. Pre-registration encouraged. Register now

title: Paint & Nosh
description: Join us for an inspiring evening of art, community, and connection at Paint and Nosh! Set against the scenic backdrop of the Farm, this event invites you to explore your creativity while enjoying delicious refreshments. Guided by guest artist Suchita and our knowledgeable Farm Educators, you’ll create your own painting while learning about peleh—the Jewish value of wonder.

title: Music Pop-Up
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration.

title: Natural Dye Workshop for Teens
description: Natural dyes can be made from materials such as leaves, bark, flowers, fruit, minerals, and more! Creating dyes from natural materials cuts out the use of harsh chemicals that can be harmful to people and the planet. Learn how to make natural dyes using materials found at home or in your yard to tie dye a bandana to take home! Led by Aliza Zollman, a passionate student who has been moved by the environmental impacts of the textile industry and fast fashion. For teens ages 11-18 only.

title: Fun Animal Friday
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Discover new, furry friends with different animal partners every month! Pre-registration is not required. Free with Museum admission. Opportunities to pet the animals are limited and timed tickets will be provided upon check-in. **Petting is entirely contingent upon the animals’ well-being. We recommend arriving at the Museum early to maximize your chances of meeting the animals. A Hold Harmless form is required to be filled out for all guests who want to pet the animals.

description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. title: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle
description: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. What is Dayenu?Dayenu is a national Jewish movement responding to the climate crisis with hope, action, and community. Local Dayenu Circles bring people together to learn, connect, and take meaningful steps toward a more just and sustainable future. You don’t need to identify as Jewish to participate—everyone is welcome.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
description: Savor the Harvest at the December Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Chef Chuck Samuelson of Heal the Earth. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

title: Harvest to Table Cooking Class
description: Join us for an evening of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Along the way, we’ll explore the Jewish values that guide what and how we grow, deepening our connection to the land and our food. Through this hands-on experience, you’ll have the chance to harvest fresh produce, learn new culinary skills, and share a nourishing meal with fellow participants.

description: Have you wondered how you could get more involved? We are growing and need your help so we can all do more good together! title: Fun with Friends
location: Imperial Beach
description: Join us for fun unstructured activities every second and fourth Wednesday of the month from 10:30 a.m.-12:30 p.m. for adults with disabilities and their caregivers.Rotating activities can include puzzles, coloring sheets, word searches, basic crafts, board games, and sensory items! No registration required. Program starts Wednesday, February 18, 2026.The Fun With Friends program will be held in the Teen Room on May 6, May 20, and June 3, 2026, due to Community Room unavailability.

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity.

title: Fall Garden Workshop Series
description: Our Fall Workshops' theme is Soil: Join us at the Beginning. Every gardening journey starts with soil, and we will be uncovering the mysteries of soil. Learn with Christina Cranwell, our experienced Outdoor Educator who has been farming 5+ years across multiple states, and Nora Mitchell, our Outdoor Educator with 30+ years of teaching and hands-on organic farming experience.

title: Music Pop-Up
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration.

title: Chanukah on the Farm
description: Kick off your Chanukah celebration at Coastal Roots Farm! Join us for activities on the Farm including: candle and dreidel making out of natural items, harvest and taste a Farm-fresh snack, learn about photosynthesis and how to be a steward of the land. Plus! Make herb infused oil using Farm-fresh herbs. Perfect for young families with little ones under the age of 8.

title: Fiddler Sing-Along
description: In honor of the first night of Chanukah, join us for a sing-along celebration of the beloved film Fiddler on the Roof! In partnership with our neighbors at The Hive, we are excited to announce an evening on the Farm filled with tasty snacks, warm drinks, dress up, and a whole lot of singing. Enjoy root veggie latkes (with Farm veggies) made fresh by The Hive's Chef Tiffani, along with apple cider to keep you warm. There will be ample heat lamp and blankets.

title: Fun Animal Friday
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Discover new, furry friends with different animal partners every month! Pre-registration is not required. Free with Museum admission. Opportunities to pet the animals are limited and timed tickets will be provided upon check-in. **Petting is entirely contingent upon the animals’ well-being. We recommend arriving at the Museum early to maximize your chances of meeting the animals. A Hold Harmless form is required to be filled out for all guests who want to pet the animals.

title: Fields of Light: A Chanukah Celebration
description: Join Coastal Roots Farm for an intergenerational celebration of Chanukah! On the final night of Chanukah, when we uplift all eight candles and with them boundless light, we come together to honor the holiday's spirit of resilience and hope. 🗓️ Sunday, December 21 🕔 4-6 p.m. 🎫 $25/adults, $10/child This event is expected to sell out! Pre-registration required. Unfortunately, walk-ups will not be accepted. During this time of heightened awareness, please expect additional security.

description: Join us at Turtle Lagoon to learn about green sea turtles, even the ones you can find right here in San Diego Bay! A Living Coast educator will tell you all about sea turtles, like their diet, shell, fun facts, and more! *All animal presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

title: Volunteer to Support Us at Our Office!
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Have you wondered how you could get more involved? We are growing and need your help so we can all do more good together! We are looking for amazing volunteers to support our field prep and office work. We can verify community service hours (including court-ordered hours) for your time at these events.

title: Music Pop-Up
location: Children’s Museum of Discovery, Escondido, CA
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration.

title: Stay Classy O’Side: Martin Luther King Jr. Park Cleanup
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and Green Oceanside for a litter cleanup at MLK Jr. Park! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. All supplies will be provided. Click below for more details and to register for the event!
